model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: SMU is the stronger predictive team by a clear margin in both KenPom and Torvik, with a notably better offense and a higher overall efficiency profile than Miami (OH). Miami (OH) has a real upset path after its outstanding season, but the RedHawks’ weaker defensive metrics and the jump in competition make SMU the safer choice in a one-game setting. I did not find a major availability issue strong enough to change that outlook.

reasoningStep.title: SMU over Miami (OH)
reasoningStep.summary: SMU has the better underlying profile, especially on offense, and no major injury news surfaced to materially alter the projection. Miami (OH) is good enough to make this competitive, but its weaker defensive indicators make the underdog case less convincing against a stronger opponent.
evidence: KenPom: SMU No. 42 (AdjEM +18.11, AdjO 123.0, AdjD 104.8) vs Miami (OH) No. 93 (AdjEM +8.25, AdjO 116.8, AdjD 108.6).
Torvik: SMU No. 42 with offense ranked 26th and defense 95th; Miami (OH) No. 87 with offense 68th and defense 149th.
News and web checks did not reveal a major availability concern for either team that would outweigh the efficiency gap.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Miami (Ohio) March Madness opponent is SMU in 2026 NCAA bracket Skip to main content Home Reds Bengals FC Cincinnati UC Xavier SportsbookWire Kentucky Ohio Miami basketball's March Madness opponent is SMU to open 2026 NCAA Tournament Chase Souder Cincinnati Enquirer Show Caption Hide Caption Travis Steele Miami RedHawks basketball coach talks ignoring critics After losing to UMass in the MAC Tournament quarterfinals, Miami head coach Travis Steele discussed how he responds to critics. It's official: The Miami RedHawks are dancing for the first time since 2007. The RedHawks are set to face 11-seeded Southern Methodist University (SMU) in the First Four of the 2026 NCAA Tournament at UD Arena. The game will be played on Wednesday, March 18. The time is to-be-determined. Miami University men's basketball wrapped up a record-shattering regular season in 2025-2026 . At 31-0 before the Mid-American Conference Tournament, the RedHawks became just the 25th team to ever complete an undefeated regular season. The RedHawks set program records for longest overall winning streak, home winning streak, road winning streak and most wins in a season. Miami also won the MAC regular-season title , the program's first title in 21 years. While many teams experienced significant roster overhaul in the offseason, the RedHawks were able to retain nearly all of their key players from last season. Led by 2026 Mid-American Conference Player of the Year Peter Suder , Miami had seven players average double-figure scoring; all but one were returners from a year ago. Travis Steele was able to protect his roster from transfer-portal raiding and it paid off. Steele was named the MAC Coach of the Year , is a finalist for two national Coach of the Year Awards, and led his RedHawks to the NCAA Tournament. Miami heard its name called on Selection Sunday, signaling the school's 18th trip to March Madness. Here's who Miami University will play in the First Four of the NCAA Tournament. What seed is Miami (OH) in NCAA Tournament? The Miami RedHawks are an 11 seed in the Midwest Region of the NCAA Tournament. Who does Miami (Ohio) play in 2026 NCAA Tournament? The RedHawks are set to face the 11-seeded Southern Methodist University (SMU) in the First Four of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. The game will be played on Wednesday, March 18. The time is to-be-determined. Buy Miami (OH) NCAA Tournament tickets Miami received its first at-large bid to the NCAA Tournament since 1999. The RedHawks reached the Sweet 16 that year, led by star player Wally Szczerbiak. SMS Facebook Twitter Email Share your feedback to help improve our site! modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
